title: Positional Encoding
summary: Self-attention is permutation-invariant, so position must be injected. Sine/cosine at different frequencies create unique position vectors with constant norm, relative position via rotation matrices, and length generalization.
key_insights: Attention has no inherent notion of token order — PE is mandatory for sequential data
Sine/cosine chosen for: unique vectors, relative position via linear combination, length generalization
Modern architectures often use learned PE — but limited to max training length
For non-sequential data (tabular, graphs), PE may be unnecessary or harmful

derivations.title: Relative position via linear transformation

steps.name: Trigonometric identity
steps.math: $\sin(a+b) = \sin a \cos b + \cos a \sin b$, $\cos(a+b) = \cos a \cos b - \sin a \sin b$

derivations.consequence: Relative position offset $k$ is a fixed rotation of $\text{PE}(\text{pos})$. Model can learn relative positions through linear operations in Q/K projections.

derivations.title: Constant norm proof
derivations.consequence: No position has a 'louder' encoding. Position information doesn't dominate or get drowned based on sequence location.

derivations.title: Dot product between positions

steps.name: Key property
steps.math: Depends ONLY on the difference $(\text{pos}_1 - \text{pos}_2)$, not absolute positions.

steps.name: Smoothness
steps.math: Small $|\Delta|$: $\cos(\omega_i \Delta) \approx 1$ → high dot product (nearby = similar). Large $|\Delta|$: cosines oscillate → lower dot product.

derivations.consequence: Adjacent positions similar, distant positions dissimilar. Attention can implement relative position sensitivity.

derivations.title: Signal preservation after addition

steps.name: Typical initialization
steps.math: $E$ initialized Xavier: $\text{std} \approx 1/\sqrt{d}$, so $\|E\| \approx 1$. But $\|\text{PE}\| = \sqrt{d/2} \approx 16$ for $d = 512$. Large ratio!

derivations.consequence: At init, PE magnitude can dominate content. Some architectures scale embeddings by $\sqrt{d_{\text{model}}}$ to balance.

geometric_properties: Dot product depends only on position DIFFERENCE — translation invariance
Monitor $\|\text{PE}\|$ vs $\|E_{\text{token}}\|$ — ratio matters for signal preservation
statistical_properties: After adding PE, mean and variance of $X$ should be uniform across positions
If PE shifts the mean significantly, subsequent LayerNorm must compensate
numerical_properties: Sine/cosine bounded in $[-1, 1]$ — no overflow risk
Learned PE: matrix $[n_{\max}, d_{\text{model}}]$ — cannot handle $n > n_{\max}$ at inference
Low-frequency (high $i$): coarse position. High-frequency (low $i$): fine position.

related: TRANSFORMER.SELF_ATTENTION — PE needed because attention is permutation-invariant
Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) — modern alternative encoding relative position directly in Q/K

notes: Rotation matrix interpretation connects to linear algebra intuition
